summary: Completed a read-only node lifecycle audit focused on merge, promote, exclude/hold, delete-candidate, quarantine, duplicate handling, and bloat prevention. Findings: lifecycle is manually invoked rather than part of finish_task; pending candidate/review backlog is not pruned or scored; lifecycle dry-run still writes reports/events; strict JSON loading can block lifecycle on malformed accepted records; compounding creates many pending review candidates and only cleans accepted behavior rules.
decisions: Did not run apply_node_lifecycle because even apply=false writes a lifecycle report and event.
Used static source inspection plus read-only data-vault counts instead of npm verification because node/npm are not on PATH in this shell.
Current user instruction to avoid file edits governed the audit.
next_steps: Add lifecycle coverage for stale pending candidates/reviews and near-duplicate candidates, or gate auto-compounding volume before review queues grow.
Make apply_node_lifecycle apply=false non-mutating or rename it to make report-writing explicit.
Make lifecycle and graph-health loaders tolerate/report malformed records instead of aborting the whole pass.
search_queries: node lifecycle merge promote quarantine duplicate bloat
candidate review queue pending behavior rule
